2026-04-17T12:42:57.0726461Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T12:42:57.0726883Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T12:42:57.0727249Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T12:42:57.0727555Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T12:42:57.0727785Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T12:42:57.0727937Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-17T12:42:57.0728505Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T12:42:57.0729234Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-17T12:46:06.3406694Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T12:44:01.143826973Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58287,\"title\":\"[BUG] TaskCanceledException are reported when using Azure Monitor and HttpClient in .NET 10\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore 1.4.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nIn a Blazor project we use HttpClient to call various services.\\nThose are cancelled if user tries to do the same thing twice, this leads to reported TaskCanceledException in Azure Application Insights. This only happens in .NET 10, not in .NET 8.\\nIf other kind of async tasks are Cancelled, those are not reported.\\n\\nOr is there any way to filter out TaskCanceledException when reported from the HttpClient?\\nOr have I missed some setting?\\nOr is this an issue with the HttpClient?\\n\\nFor us, this leads to a substantial amount of reported Exceptions that should be ignored, also leads to Azure Monitor reporting lots of false Alert Exception Anomalies...\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nSince a normal TaskCanceledException is not reported, we did not expect TaskCanceledException from the HttpClient to be reported.\\nAlso in .NET 8, it is not reported, as expected.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThis is reported (sorry about the Swedish text in the stack trace):\\nSystem.Threading.Tasks.TaskCanceledException: The operation was canceled.\\n ---\\u0026gt; System.IO.IOException: Unable to read data from the transport connection: I/O-åtgärden har avbrutits därför att en tråd har avslutats eller för att ett program har begärt det..\\n ---\\u0026gt; System.Net.Sockets.SocketException (995): I/O-åtgärden har avbrutits därför att en tråd har avslutats eller för att ett program har begärt det.\\n   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---\\n   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs.ThrowException(SocketError error, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs.System.Threading.Tasks.Sources.IValueTaskSource.GetResult(Int16 token)\\n   at System.Net.Http.HttpConnection.InitialFillAsync(Boolean async)\\n   at System.Net.Http.HttpConnection.SendAsync(HttpRequestMessage request, Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---\\n   at System.Net.Http.HttpConnection.SendAsync(HttpRequestMessage request, Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   at System.Net.Http.HttpConnectionPool.SendWithVersionDetectionAndRetryAsync(HttpRequestMessage request, Boolean async, Boolean doRequestAuth, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   at System.Net.Http.Metrics.MetricsHandler.SendAsyncWithMetrics(HttpRequestMessage request, Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   at System.Net.Http.DiagnosticsHandler.SendAsyncCore(HttpRequestMessage request, Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nCreate new Blazor Server .NET 10 project with sample pages.\\nAdd reference to: Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore 1.4.0\\n\\nIn Program.cs (replace [MyKey]:\\n`builder.Services.AddHttpClient();\\n\\nbuilder.Services\\n\\t.AddOpenTelemetry()\\n\\t.UseAzureMonitor(options =\\u0026gt; field ?? throw new Exception(\\u0026#34;Property HttpClient is not initialized\\u0026#34;); set; }\\n\\nprivate async Task IncrementCount()\\n{\\n    if (_cancellationTokenSource != null)\\n        await _cancellationTokenSource.CancelAsync();\\n    _cancellationTokenSource = new CancellationTokenSource();\\n\\n   await this.HttpClient.GetAsync(\\u0026#34;http://localhost:[MyPort]/test\\u0026#34;, _cancellationTokenSource.Token);\\n\\n   // await Task.Delay(5000, _cancellationTokenSource.Token);\\n\\n    _cancellationTokenSource.Dispose();\\n    _cancellationTokenSource = null;\\n    \\n    currentCount++;\\n}`\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nWindows 11\\n.NET 10.0.6\\nVisual Studio 18.5.0\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58287\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"andreascaesar\",\"id\":8454845,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/andreascaesar\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8454845?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T12:41:28Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T12:41:28Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-17T12:46:06.3587488Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T12:45:54.060422775Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** In a Blazor Server project on .NET 10, `TaskCanceledException` thrown by `HttpClient` (when a request is explicitly cancelled) is being reported as an exception in Azure Application Insights via `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore 1.4.0`, whereas in .NET 8 the same scenario does not produce a reported exception.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Details\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore 1.4.0`\n**Affected API:** `UseAzureMonitor` / OpenTelemetry exception reporting pipeline for `HttpClient`\n**Scenarios:**\n- User triggers the same Blazor action twice; second trigger cancels the in-flight `HttpClient` request via `CancellationTokenSource.CancelAsync()`\n- The resulting `TaskCanceledException` (wrapping `IOException`/`SocketException`) is captured and reported to Azure Monitor as an exception\n- On .NET 8 the same cancellation flow does NOT produce a reported exception; on .NET 10 it does\n- Non-HTTP `Task.Delay` cancellations are NOT reported, confirming the difference is specific to `HttpClient` instrumentation\n\n**Root ask:** Either suppress `TaskCanceledException` from `HttpClient` cancellations from being reported to Azure Monitor (matching .NET 8 behavior), or provide a configuration option to filter them out.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe exception originates deep in `System.Net.Http.HttpConnection` (socket-level I/O abort) and surfaces as a `TaskCanceledException` wrapping `IOException → SocketException (995)`. The stack trace shows it comes from the `MetricsHandler` / `DiagnosticsHandler` pipeline in `System.Net.Http`, suggesting .NET 10 may have changed how `HttpClient` propagates cancellation (switching from a clean `OperationCanceledException` to a socket-level abort path), causing the OpenTelemetry `HttpClient` instrumentation to treat it as an error rather than a cancellation.\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1. Compare how `OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.HttpClient` (transitively used by the Distro) classifies `TaskCanceledException` on .NET 8 vs .NET 10 — specifically whether the `IsCanceled` flag or exception type check changed.\n2. Check whether `System.Net.Http.DiagnosticsHandler` on .NET 10 fires the `HttpHandlerDiagnosticListener` error event for socket-abort cancellations differently than .NET 8.\n3. Evaluate adding a filter/sampler in the Distro that suppresses exception telemetry for `TaskCanceledException` instances where `CancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested` is true or `HttpRequestException.StatusCode` is absent.\n4. As a workaround for the customer: wrap the `HttpClient.GetAsync` call in a `try/catch` for `OperationCanceledException` and re-throw only if not cancelled by user intent; or configure an `ActivitySource` filter to suppress reporting of activities with `Status == Error` caused by cancellation.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🏷️ Label Confidence\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore` is a data plane client library (not `Azure.ResourceManager.*` / `Azure.Provisioning.*`), so \"Client\" applies.\n- **Service:** `Monitor - Distro` — CODEOWNERS maps `/sdk/monitor/Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore/` to the `%Monitor - Distro` service label; the package is the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry \"Distro\" (distribution) package for ASP.NET Core.\n- **Confidence:** High — the package name is explicitly stated in the issue, the CODEOWNERS mapping is unambiguous, and a quality reference issue (#37991: \"Client\" + \"Monitor - Exporter\") confirms the label pattern for Monitor OpenTelemetry packages.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e👥 Owner Routing\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Monitor - Distro` (line 357) — matched because the predicted service label is \"Monitor - Distro\", which corresponds exactly to this entry.\n- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed\n- **ServiceOwners:** @harsimar @mattsains-msft @rajkumar-rangaraj @xiang17\n- **Routing action:** Added \"Service Attention\" and \"needs-team-attention\" labels; issue left unassigned (no AzureSdkOwners); ServiceOwners mentioned via routing comment.\n- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top. Entries for `%Monitor` catch-all (line 1047 and 348) were encountered but skipped because `%Monitor - Distro` at line 357 matched first and is the more specific entry. Entries for `%Monitor - Exporter`, `%Monitor - Query`, `%Monitor - ApplicationInsights`, `%Monitor - LiveMetrics` all require different service labels not present in this issue.\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":"58287"},"name":"add_comment"}}}

Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully."
2026-04-17T12:46:06.3899237Z       },
2026-04-17T12:46:06.3899307Z       {
2026-04-17T12:46:06.3899393Z         "name": "mention_owners",
2026-04-17T12:46:06.3899795Z         "description": "Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization"
2026-04-17T12:46:06.3899862Z       }
2026-04-17T12:46:06.3899926Z     ]
